verses.latin_vulgate: Audite me, qui sequimini quod justum est,  et quæritis Dominum ;  attendite ad petram unde excisi estis,  et ad cavernam laci de qua præcisi estis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֥וּ אֵלַ֛י רֹ֥דְפֵי צֶ֖דֶק מְבַקְשֵׁ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה הַבִּ֙יטוּ֙ אֶל־צ֣וּר חֻצַּבְתֶּ֔ם וְאֶל־מַקֶּ֥בֶת בּ֖וֹר נֻקַּרְתֶּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Give ear to me, you that follow that which is just, and you that seek the Lord: look unto the rock whence you are hewn, and to the hole of the pit from which you are dug out.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuulkaat minua te, jotka vanhurskautta noudatatte, te jotka Herraa etsitte. Katsokaat sitä kalliota, josta te lohaistut olette, ja sitä kaivoa, josta te kaivetut olette.
verses.king_james: Hearken to me, ye that follow after righteousness, ye that seek the Lord: look unto the rock whence ye are hewn, and to the hole of the pit whence ye are digged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendite ad Abraham, patrem vestrum,  et ad Saram, quæ peperit vos :  quia unum vocavi eum,  et benedixi ei, et multiplicavi eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַבִּ֙יטוּ֙ אֶל־אַבְרָהָ֣ם אֲבִיכֶ֔ם וְאֶל־שָׂרָ֖ה תְּחוֹלֶלְכֶ֑ם כִּֽי־אֶחָ֣ד קְרָאתִ֔יו וַאֲבָרְכֵ֖הוּ וְאַרְבֵּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Look unto Abraham your father, and to Sara that bore you: for I called him alone, and blessed him, and multiplied him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katsokaat Abrahamia teidän isäänne, ja Saaraa, joka teidät synnytti; sillä minä kutsuin hänen, kuin hän oli vielä yksinäinen, siunasin ja enensin hänen.
verses.king_james: Look unto Abraham your father, and unto Sarah that bare you: for I called him alone, and blessed him, and increased him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consolabitur ergo Dominus Sion,  et consolabitur omnes ruinas ejus :  et ponet desertum ejus quasi delicias,  et solitudinem ejus quasi hortum Domini.  Gaudium et lætitia invenietur in ea,  gratiarum actio et vox laudis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־נִחַ֨ם יְהֹוָ֜ה צִיּ֗וֹן נִחַם֙ כׇּל־חׇרְבֹתֶ֔יהָ וַיָּ֤שֶׂם מִדְבָּרָהּ֙ כְּעֵ֔דֶן וְעַרְבָתָ֖הּ כְּגַן־יְהֹוָ֑ה שָׂשׂ֤וֹן וְשִׂמְחָה֙ יִמָּ֣צֵא בָ֔הּ תּוֹדָ֖ה וְק֥וֹל זִמְרָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: The Lord therefore will comfort Sion, and will comfort all the ruins thereof: and he will make her desert as a place of pleasure, and her wilderness as the garden of the Lord. Joy and gladness shall be found therein, thanksgiving, and the voice of praise.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Herra lohduttaa Zionia, hän lohduttaa kaiken hänen aukiutensa, ja tekee hänen aukiutensa niinkuin Edenin, ja hänen erämaansa niinkuin Herran yrttitarhan; että siellä löytään ilo ja riemu, kiitos ja veisun ääni.
verses.king_james: For the Lord shall comfort Zion: he will comfort all her waste places; and he will make her wilderness like Eden, and her desert like the garden of the Lord; joy and gladness shall be found therein, thanksgiving, and the voice of melody.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendite ad me, popule meus,  et tribus mea, me audite :  quia lex a me exiet,  et judicium meum in lucem populorum requiescet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַקְשִׁ֤יבוּ אֵלַי֙ עַמִּ֔י וּלְאוּמִּ֖י אֵלַ֣י הַאֲזִ֑ינוּ כִּ֤י תוֹרָה֙ מֵאִתִּ֣י תֵצֵ֔א וּמִ֨שְׁפָּטִ֔י לְא֥וֹר עַמִּ֖ים אַרְגִּֽיעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hearken unto me, O my people, and give ear to me, O my tribes: for a law shall go forth from me, and my judgment shall rest to be a light of the nations.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ottakaat minusta vaari, minun kansani, ja kuulkaat minua minun sukukuntani; sillä minulta on laki käyvä ulos, ja minä panen oikeuteni kansoille valkeudeksi.
verses.king_james: Hearken unto me, my people; and give ear unto me, O my nation: for a law shall proceed from me, and I will make my judgment to rest for a light of the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prope est justus meus, egressus est salvator meus,  et brachia mea populos judicabunt ;  me insulæ exspectabunt,  et brachium meum sustinebunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קָר֤וֹב צִדְקִי֙ יָצָ֣א יִשְׁעִ֔י וּזְרֹעַ֖י עַמִּ֣ים יִשְׁפֹּ֑טוּ אֵלַי֙ אִיִּ֣ים יְקַוּ֔וּ וְאֶל־זְרֹעִ֖י יְיַחֵלֽוּן׃
verses.douay_rheims: My just one is near at hand, my saviour is gone forth, and my arms shall judge the people: the islands shall look for me, and shall patiently wait for my arm.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun vanhurskauteni on läsnä, minun autuuteni käy ulos, ja minun käsivarteni on kansoja tuomitseva. Luodot odottavat minua, ja vartioitsevat minun käsivarttani.
verses.king_james: My righteousness is near; my salvation is gone forth, and mine arms shall judge the people; the isles shall wait upon me, and on mine arm shall they trust.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levate in cælum oculos vestros,  et videte sub terra deorsum :  quia cæli sicut fumus liquescent,  et terra sicut vestimentum atteretur,  et habitatores ejus sicut hæc interibunt :  salus autem mea in sempiternum erit,  et justitia mea non deficiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂאוּ֩ לַשָּׁמַ֨יִם עֵינֵיכֶ֜ם וְֽהַבִּ֧יטוּ אֶל־הָאָ֣רֶץ מִתַּ֗חַת כִּֽי־שָׁמַ֜יִם כֶּעָשָׁ֤ן נִמְלָ֙חוּ֙ וְהָאָ֙רֶץ֙ כַּבֶּ֣גֶד תִּבְלֶ֔ה וְיֹשְׁבֶ֖יהָ כְּמוֹ־כֵ֣ן יְמוּת֑וּן וִישֽׁוּעָתִי֙ לְעוֹלָ֣ם תִּֽהְיֶ֔ה וְצִדְקָתִ֖י לֹ֥א תֵחָֽת׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Lift up your eyes to heaven, and look down to the earth beneath: for the heavens shall vanish like smoke, and the earth shall be worn away like a garment, and the inhabitants thereof shall perish in like manner: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice shall not fail.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nostakaat teidän silmänne taivaasen, ja katsokaat alas maan päälle; sillä taivas on katoova niinkuin savu, ja maa vanhenee niinkuin vaate, niin myös sen asuvaiset kuolevat; mutta minun autuuteni pysyy ijankaikkisesti, ja minun vanhurskauteni ei ole puuttuva.
verses.king_james: Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look upon the earth beneath: for the heavens shall vanish away like smoke, and the earth shall wax old like a garment, and they that dwell therein shall die in like manner: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my righteousness shall not be abolished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite me, qui scitis justum,  populus meus, lex mea in corde eorum :  nolite timere opprobrium hominum,  et blasphemias eorum ne metuatis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֤וּ אֵלַי֙ יֹ֣דְעֵי צֶ֔דֶק עַ֖ם תּוֹרָתִ֣י בְלִבָּ֑ם אַל־תִּֽירְאוּ֙ חֶרְפַּ֣ת אֱנ֔וֹשׁ וּמִגִּדֻּפֹתָ֖ם אַל־תֵּחָֽתּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hearken to me, you that know what is just, my people who have my law in your heart: fear ye not the reproach of men, and be not afraid of their blasphemies.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuulkaat minua te, jotka vanhurskauden tunnette, kansa, jonka sydämessä minun lakini on. Älkäät peljätkö, kussa ihmiset teitä häpäisevät, ja älkäät vavisko, kuin he teitä pilkkaavat.
verses.king_james: Hearken unto me, ye that know righteousness, the people in whose heart is my law; fear ye not the reproach of men, neither be ye afraid of their revilings.

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut enim vestimentum, sic comedet eos vermis,  et sicut lanam, sic devorabit eos tinea :  salus autem mea in sempiternum erit,  et justitia mea in generationes generationum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י כַבֶּ֙גֶד֙ יֹאכְלֵ֣ם עָ֔שׁ וְכַצֶּ֖מֶר יֹאכְלֵ֣ם סָ֑ס וְצִדְקָתִי֙ לְעוֹלָ֣ם תִּֽהְיֶ֔ה וִישׁוּעָתִ֖י לְד֥וֹר דּוֹרִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: For the worm shall eat them up as a garment: and the moth shall consume them as wool: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice from generation to generation.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä koin pitää heitä syömän niinkuin vaatetta, ja madot pitää heidän syömän niinkuin villaisia; mutta minun vanhurskauteni pysyy ijankaikkisesti, ja minun autuuteni suvusta sukuun.
verses.king_james: For the moth shall eat them up like a garment, and the worm shall eat them like wool: but my righteousness shall be for ever, and my salvation from generation to generation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consurge, consurge, induere fortitudinem,  brachium Domini !  consurge sicut in diebus antiquis,  in generationibus sæculorum.  Numquid non tu percussisti superbum,  vulnerasti draconem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עוּרִ֨י עוּרִ֤י לִבְשִׁי־עֹז֙ זְר֣וֹעַ יְהֹוָ֔ה ע֚וּרִי כִּ֣ימֵי קֶ֔דֶם דֹּר֖וֹת עוֹלָמִ֑ים הֲל֥וֹא אַתְּ־הִ֛יא הַמַּחְצֶ֥בֶת רַ֖הַב מְחוֹלֶ֥לֶת תַּנִּֽין׃
verses.douay_rheims: Arise, arise, put on strength, O thou arm of the Lord, arise as in the days of old, in the ancient generations. Hast not thou struck the proud one, and wounded the dragon?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ylös, ylös, pue päälles väkevyyttä, sinä Herran käsivarsi; ylös, niinkuin muinen vanhaan aikaan. Etkös se ole, joka löit ylpiän, ja haavoitit lohikärmeen?
verses.king_james: Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of the Lord; awake, as in the ancient days, in the generations of old. Art thou not it that hath cut Rahab, and wounded the dragon?

verses.latin_vulgate: numquid non tu siccasti mare,  aquam abyssi vehementis ;  qui posuisti profundum maris viam,  ut transirent liberati ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲל֤וֹא אַתְּ־הִיא֙ הַמַּחֲרֶ֣בֶת יָ֔ם מֵ֖י תְּה֣וֹם רַבָּ֑ה הַשָּׂ֙מָה֙ מַֽעֲמַקֵּי־יָ֔ם דֶּ֖רֶךְ לַעֲבֹ֥ר גְּאוּלִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hast not thou dried up the sea, the water of the mighty deep, who madest the depth of the sea a way, that the delivered might pass over?
verses.finnish_biblia: Etkös se ole, joka meren, suuren syvän veden kuivasit? joka syvän meren tieksi teit, että lunastetut kävivät sitä myöten.
verses.king_james: Art thou not it which hath dried the sea, the waters of the great deep; that hath made the depths of the sea a way for the ransomed to pass over?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc qui redempti sunt a Domino, revertentur,  et venient in Sion laudantes,  et lætitia sempiterna super capita eorum :  gaudium et lætitiam tenebunt ;  fugiet dolor et gemitus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּפְדוּיֵ֨י יְהֹוָ֜ה יְשׁוּב֗וּן וּבָ֤אוּ צִיּוֹן֙ בְּרִנָּ֔ה וְשִׂמְחַ֥ת עוֹלָ֖ם עַל־רֹאשָׁ֑ם שָׂשׂ֤וֹן וְשִׂמְחָה֙ יַשִּׂיג֔וּן נָ֖סוּ יָג֥וֹן וַאֲנָחָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And now they that are redeemed by the Lord, shall return, and shall come into Sion singing praises, and joy everlasting shall be upon their heads, they shall obtain joy and gladness, sorrow and mourning shall flee away.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Herran lunastetut palajavat ja tulevat Zioniin riemulla, ja ijankaikkinen ilo on heidän päänsä päällä oleva; ilon ja riemun he käsittävät, murhe ja huokaus pakenee heitä.
verses.king_james: Therefore the redeemed of the Lord shall return, and come with singing unto Zion; and everlasting joy shall be upon their head: they shall obtain gladness and joy; and sorrow and mourning shall flee away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego, ego ipse consolabor vos.  Quis tu, ut timeres ab homine mortali,  et a filio hominis qui quasi fœnum ita arescet ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָנֹכִ֧י אָנֹכִ֛י ה֖וּא מְנַחֶמְכֶ֑ם מִי־אַ֤תְּ וַתִּֽירְאִי֙ מֵאֱנ֣וֹשׁ יָמ֔וּת וּמִבֶּן־אָדָ֖ם חָצִ֥יר יִנָּתֵֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: I myself will comfort you: who art thou, that thou shouldst be afraid of a mortal man, and of the son of man, who shall wither away like grass?
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä, minä olen teidän lohduttajanne; kuka siis sinä olet, ettäs kuolevaista ihmistä pelkäät, ja ihmisten lapsia, jotka kulutetaan niinkuin heinä?
verses.king_james: I, even I, am he that comforteth you: who art thou, that thou shouldest be afraid of a man that shall die, and of the son of man which shall be made as grass;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et oblitus es Domini, factoris tui,  qui tetendit cælos et fundavit terram ;  et formidasti jugiter tota die  a facie furoris ejus qui te tribulabat,  et paraverat ad perdendum.  Ubi nunc est furor tribulantis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּשְׁכַּ֞ח יְהֹוָ֣ה עֹשֶׂ֗ךָ נוֹטֶ֣ה שָׁמַ֘יִם֮ וְיֹסֵ֣ד אָ֒רֶץ֒ וַתְּפַחֵ֨ד תָּמִ֜יד כׇּל־הַיּ֗וֹם מִפְּנֵי֙ חֲמַ֣ת הַמֵּצִ֔יק כַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר כּוֹנֵ֖ן לְהַשְׁחִ֑ית וְאַיֵּ֖ה חֲמַ֥ת הַמֵּצִֽיק׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thou hast forgotten the Lord thy maker, who stretched out the heavens, and founded the earth: and thou hast been afraid continually all the day at the presence of his fury who afflicted thee, and had prepared himself to destroy thee: where is now the fury of the oppressor?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja unohdat Herran, joka sinun tehnyt on, joka taivaat levittää, ja maan perustaa? Mutta sinä pelkäät alati yli päivää vaivaajan julmuutta, kuin hän rupee hukuttamaan. Ja kuhunka jäi vaivaajan julmuus?
verses.king_james: And forgettest the Lord thy maker, that hath stretched forth the heavens, and laid the foundations of the earth; and hast feared continually every day because of the fury of the oppressor, as if he were ready to destroy? and where is the fury of the oppressor?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cito veniet gradiens ad aperiendum ;  et non interficiet usque ad internecionem,  nec deficiet panis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִהַ֥ר צֹעֶ֖ה לְהִפָּתֵ֑חַ וְלֹא־יָמ֣וּת לַשַּׁ֔חַת וְלֹ֥א יֶחְסַ֖ר לַחְמֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He shall quickly come that is going to open unto you, and he shall not kill unto utter destruction, neither shall his bread fail.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska hän vaadittiin rientämään ja juoksemaan ympäri, että hän päästäis, ettei he kuolisi kadotukseen, eikä hänen leipänsä puuttuisi?
verses.king_james: The captive exile hasteneth that he may be loosed, and that he should not die in the pit, nor that his bread should fail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem sum Dominus Deus tuus,  qui conturbo mare, et intumescunt fluctus ejus :  Dominus exercituum nomen meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָֽנֹכִי֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֶ֔יךָ רֹגַ֣ע הַיָּ֔ם וַיֶּהֱמ֖וּ גַּלָּ֑יו יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת שְׁמֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But I am the Lord thy God, who trouble the sea, and the waves thereof swell: the Lord of hosts is my name.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minä olen Herra sinun Jumalas, joka meren liikutan, että sen aallot pauhaavat: hänen nimensä on Herra Zebaot.
verses.king_james: But I am the Lord thy God, that divided the sea, whose waves roared: The Lord of hosts is his name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posui verba mea in ore tuo,  et in umbra manus meæ protexi te,  ut plantes cælos, et fundes terram,  et dicas ad Sion : Populus meus es tu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאָשִׂ֤ם דְּבָרַי֙ בְּפִ֔יךָ וּבְצֵ֥ל יָדִ֖י כִּסִּיתִ֑יךָ לִנְטֹ֤עַ שָׁמַ֙יִם֙ וְלִיסֹ֣ד אָ֔רֶץ וְלֵאמֹ֥ר לְצִיּ֖וֹן עַמִּי־אָֽתָּה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: I have put my words in thy mouth, and have protected thee in the shadow of my hand, that thou mightest plant the heavens, and found the earth: and mightest say to Sion: Thou art my people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä panen sanani sinun suuhus, ja peitän sinun käteni varjolla, että minä taivaan istuttaisin, ja maan perustaisin ja sanoisin Zionille: sinä olet minun kansani.
verses.king_james: And I have put my words in thy mouth, and I have covered thee in the shadow of mine hand, that I may plant the heavens, and lay the foundations of the earth, and say unto Zion, Thou art my people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Elevare, elevare,  consurge, Jerusalem,  quæ bibisti de manu Domini  calicem iræ ejus ;  usque ad fundum calicis soporis bibisti,  et potasti usque ad fæces.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִתְעוֹרְרִ֣י הִֽתְעוֹרְרִ֗י ק֚וּמִי יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁתִ֛ית מִיַּ֥ד יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶת־כּ֣וֹס חֲמָת֑וֹ אֶת־קֻבַּ֜עַת כּ֧וֹס הַתַּרְעֵלָ֛ה שָׁתִ֖ית מָצִֽית׃
verses.douay_rheims: Arise, arise, stand up, O Jerusalem, which hast drunk at the hand of the Lord the cup of his wrath; thou hast drunk even to the bottom of the cup of dead sleep, and thou hast drunk even to the dregs.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herää, herää, nouse Jerusalem, joka joit Herran kädestä hänen vihansa kalkin, sen kompastuskalkin rahkan olet sinä juonut ja särkenyt ulos.
verses.king_james: Awake, awake, stand up, O Jerusalem, which hast drunk at the hand of the Lord the cup of his fury; thou hast drunken the dregs of the cup of trembling, and wrung them out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est qui sustentet eam,  ex omnibus filiis quos genuit ;  et non est qui apprehendat manum ejus,  ex omnibus filiis quos enutrivit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵין־מְנַהֵ֣ל לָ֔הּ מִכׇּל־בָּנִ֖ים יָלָ֑דָה וְאֵ֤ין מַחֲזִיק֙ בְּיָדָ֔הּ מִכׇּל־בָּנִ֖ים גִּדֵּֽלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: There is none that can uphold her among all the children that she hath brought forth: and there is none that taketh her by the hand among all the children that she hath brought up.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei kenkään hänen pojistansa, jotka hän oli synnyttänyt, häntä holhonnut: eikä kenkään kaikista hänen lapsistansa, jotka hän oli kasvattanut, hänen käteensä ruvennut.
verses.king_james: There is none to guide her among all the sons whom she hath brought forth; neither is there any that taketh her by the hand of all the sons that she hath brought up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duo sunt quæ occurrerunt tibi ;  quis contristabitur super te ?  Vastitas, et contritio, et fames, et gladius ;  quis consolabitur te ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁתַּ֤יִם הֵ֙נָּה֙ קֹרְאֹתַ֔יִךְ מִ֖י יָנ֣וּד לָ֑ךְ הַשֹּׁ֧ד וְהַשֶּׁ֛בֶר וְהָרָעָ֥ב וְהַחֶ֖רֶב מִ֥י אֲנַחֲמֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: There are two things that have happened to thee: who shall be sorry for thee? desolation, and destruction, and the famine, and the sword, who shall comfort thee?
verses.finnish_biblia: Nämät kaksi sinua kohtasivat, kuka sinua armahti? hävitys, vahinko, nälkä ja miekka: millä minä sinua lohdutan?
verses.king_james: These two things are come unto thee; who shall be sorry for thee? desolation, and destruction, and the famine, and the sword: by whom shall I comfort thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii tui projecti sunt, dormierunt  in capite omnium viarum  sicut oryx illaqueatus,  pleni indignatione Domini,  increpatione Dei tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּנַ֜יִךְ עֻלְּפ֥וּ שָׁכְב֛וּ בְּרֹ֥אשׁ כׇּל־חוּצ֖וֹת כְּת֣וֹא מִכְמָ֑ר הַֽמְלֵאִ֥ים חֲמַת־יְהֹוָ֖ה גַּעֲרַ֥ת אֱלֹהָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thy children are cast forth, they have slept at the head of all the ways, and the wild ox that is snared: full of the indignation of the Lord, of the rebuke of thy God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun lapses olivat pintuneet ja makasivat kaikissa katujen päissä, niinkuin paulalla sidottu metsähärkä, täynnä Herran vihaa ja sinun Jumalas rangaistusta.
verses.king_james: Thy sons have fainted, they lie at the head of all the streets, as a wild bull in a net: they are full of the fury of the Lord, the rebuke of thy God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Idcirco audi hoc, paupercula,  et ebria non a vino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֛ן שִׁמְעִי־נָ֥א זֹ֖את עֲנִיָּ֑ה וּשְׁכֻרַ֖ת וְלֹ֥א מִיָּֽיִן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore hear this, thou poor little one, and thou that art drunk but not with wine.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden kuule nyt tätä sinä raadollinen, ja sinä juopunut ilman viinaa.
verses.king_james: Therefore hear now this, thou afflicted, and drunken, but not with wine:

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit dominator tuus Dominus,  et Deus tuus, qui pugnabit pro populo suo :  Ecce tuli de manu tua  calicem soporis,  fundum calicis indignationis meæ :  non adjicies ut bibas illum ultra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּה־אָמַ֞ר אֲדֹנַ֣יִךְ יְהֹוָ֗ה וֵאלֹהַ֙יִךְ֙ יָרִ֣יב עַמּ֔וֹ הִנֵּ֥ה לָקַ֛חְתִּי מִיָּדֵ֖ךְ אֶת־כּ֣וֹס הַתַּרְעֵלָ֑ה אֶת־קֻבַּ֙עַת֙ כּ֣וֹס חֲמָתִ֔י לֹא־תוֹסִ֥יפִי לִשְׁתּוֹתָ֖הּ עֽוֹד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thus saith thy Sovereign the Lord, and thy God, who will fight for his people: Behold I have taken out of thy hand the cup of dead sleep, the dregs of the cup of my indignation, thou shalt not drink it again any more.
verses.finnish_biblia: Näin sanoo Herra, sinun Herras ja sinun Jumalas, joka kansansa edestä sotii: katso, minä otan kompastuskalkin sinun kädestäs, ja minun vihani kalkin rahkan; ei sinun pidä sitä enään juoman.
verses.king_james: Thus saith thy Lord the Lord, and thy God that pleadeth the cause of his people, Behold, I have taken out of thine hand the cup of trembling, even the dregs of the cup of my fury; thou shalt no more drink it again:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ponam illum in manu eorum qui te humiliaverunt,  et dixerunt animæ tuæ :  Incurvare, ut transeamus ;  et posuisti ut terram corpus tuum,  et quasi viam transeuntibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשַׂמְתִּ֙יהָ֙ בְּיַד־מוֹגַ֔יִךְ אֲשֶׁר־אָמְר֥וּ לְנַפְשֵׁ֖ךְ שְׁחִ֣י וְנַעֲבֹ֑רָה וַתָּשִׂ֤ימִי כָאָ֙רֶץ֙ גֵּוֵ֔ךְ וְכַח֖וּץ לַעֹבְרִֽים׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And I will put it in the hand of them that have oppressed thee, and have said to thy soul: Bow down, that we may go over: and thou hast laid thy body as the ground, and as a way to them that went over.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan minä panen sen sinun raateliais käteen, jotka sanoivat sinun sielulles: kumarra sinus, että me kävisimme sinun ylitses, ja pane sinun selkäs maahan, että niinkuin katua myöten sen ylitse mentäisiin.
verses.king_james: But I will put it into the hand of them that afflict thee; which have said to thy soul, Bow down, that we may go over: and thou hast laid thy body as the ground, and as the street, to them that went over.
